concerns.text: SC-006 contains a severe scope shift and formatting error. The text states: '...with The source data (Materials Project, NREL) primarily provides experimentally measured decomposition temperatures (T_d) via thermogravimetric analysis (TGA). Post-synthesis stability measurements beyond T_d (e.g., long-term aging, humidity exposure) are NOT included in the core training set; the scope is strictly limited to thermal decomposition temperature as the primary stability metric. External validation on literature data for other stability modes is handled separately in FR-006. flagged if missing (See US-1)'. This paragraph is a copy-paste of a scope definition that was accidentally inserted into the middle of a success criterion sentence, breaking the grammatical structure and the logical flow of the requirement. It also contradicts the 'Measurable Outcomes' header by defining scope boundaries rather than a measurement metric.

Acceptance Scenario 3 states: '...at least 200 entries remain for training with ≤10% missing values in any descriptor column.' This introduces a specific empirical threshold (200 entries) that conflicts with the 'Measurable Outcomes' guidance in the Success Criteria section which explicitly states: 'Planning docs state *what* will be measured... defer specific empirical values (counts, dataset sizes, measured quantities, percentages) to the implementation/research phase.' While the Constitution (FR-030) allows for assumptions, mixing specific hard numbers in User Story acceptance criteria with the explicit instruction to defer them in Success Criteria creates a minor internal inconsistency in the spec's stance on empirical values.
